extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked solo on Passo di Ganda with 60km remaining, descended recklessly through 19 hairpins on Selvino, and held off late-chasing MASNADA to win.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR went solo 60km out on Passo di Ganda when rivals expected group tactics, forcing reactive chaos not coordinated pursuit.
MASNADA's local knowledge on Selvino descent allowed him to close 30-second gap on technical terrain, reaching POGAČAR before flatter roads.
UAE isolated POGAČAR by having BAGIOLI set extreme descent pace pre-Ganda, shedding BENOOT and weaker climbers before decisive attack.
YATES outsprinted ROGLIČ for podium, suggesting strongest climbers may lack explosiveness after chasing long-range solo moves in Lombardia finale.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Won after 60km solo including technical descent, following Liege and Tour victories same season, showing unprecedented Monument range.
rider_signals.MASNADA Fausto: positive: Local Bergamo rider bridged 30-second gap on Selvino descent alone, nearly rewrote history before sprint deficit revealed itself.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Third place ahead of ROGLIČ suggests superior freshness or sprint after chasing, unexpected podium over pre-race favorites.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Fourth despite being pre-race favorite, couldn't organize chase effectively and lost podium sprint to YATES.
rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: negative: Dropped on Ganda descent when UAE set extreme pace, revealing vulnerability on technical descending under sustained speed.
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: Confirmed bad day early, dropped before final climb, suggesting poor form or tactical miscalculation in Monument preparation.
